title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
summary: Agents claiming to search the web often fail silently by hitting CAPTCHAs, anti-bot systems, or falling back to stale training data while returning answers as if successful. A demonstration using Bright Data's Web MCP shows how real web access versus blocked access produces different results, revealing reliability issues in citations, real-time data, and hallucination rates.
writeup: Rafael Levi of Bright Data examines a common failure mode in AI agents: claiming to have searched the web while silently hitting CAPTCHAs and anti-bot systems or falling back to stale training data, then returning answers as if the search succeeded. A demonstration using Bright Data's Web MCP contrasts real web access with blocked access to show how the two produce different results. The takeaway is that this silent failure undermines reliability in citations, real-time data, and hallucination rates, making the distinction between genuine and blocked access material to agent trustworthiness.
